---
title: "Replay & Error Handling"
subtitle: "Two-invocation halt-with-replay-manifest model for recovering failed replicates"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

## Overview

Failed post-trait simulation fanout replicates no longer terminate the pipeline. A task that
exhausts its retries is dropped from its downstream channel; the surviving
reps complete to the database; and `workflow.onComplete` emits a
`replay.tsv` listing the dropped slots. A second invocation with
`-profile replay --replay <tsv> -resume` recovers them. The target
replication is achieved across two runs: the first writes the reps that
succeed (N − k of N), and the replay fills in the remaining k.

| Component | Role |
|---|---|
| `errorStrategy = { workflow.stubRun ? 'retry' : (task.attempt <= task.maxRetries ? 'retry' : 'ignore') }` | Post-fanout processes drop on retry exhaustion. Stub runs preserve retry semantics. (`conf/rockfish.config`) |
| `bin/failure_trap.sh` | Bash EXIT trap installed by each post-fanout process. Writes `${output_dir}/.failures/<key>.json` on non-zero exit. |
| `workflow.onComplete` (`main.nf`) | Aggregates the `.failures/` records and writes `${output_dir}/replay.tsv`. |
| `--replay` filter (`main.nf:462, 487`) | Restricts fanout to the 6-tuple `(species, group, nqtl, effect, h2, rep)` slots listed in the manifest. |
| `DB_CLEAN_REPLAY_SLOTS` | Deletes the failed slots' partial DB rows (12 files per slot) before the replay rewrites them. |
| `VALIDATE_REPLICATION_COMPLETE` | Emits a warning if any parameter combination is short. Short replication is **non-fatal** — the validator exits 0. |

Prep-phase processes (`bcftools_*`, `plink_recode_*`,
`r_find_genotype_matrix_eigen`) stay on `terminate`. A prep-phase failure
is cone-level (the whole `(group, maf)` subtree fails), not rep-level, so
per-rep replay is meaningless upstream of fanout — and the trap cannot
write a valid manifest record there. See
`nemascan-sims-nf-notes/Development/error-handling/planning/halt-with-replay-manifest.md`
for the design rationale.


## First run

Standard invocation — no replay-specific flags:

```bash
nextflow run main.nf -profile rockfish \
    --strainfile strains.tsv \
    --vcf 20231213 \
    --output_dir ${OUTPUT}
```

The pipeline runs to completion regardless of per-rep failures. Exit
status is **0** under `errorStrategy = 'ignore'`, even when reps were
dropped (short replication is a warning, not an error). Inspect the run:

```bash
# Per-failure JSON records (one per failed task, written by the EXIT trap)
ls ${OUTPUT}/.failures/

# Aggregated manifest (one data row per failed slot)
cat ${OUTPUT}/replay.tsv

# Columns
head -1 ${OUTPUT}/replay.tsv | tr '\t' '\n'
# session, species, group, maf, nqtl, effect, h2, rep, mode, type, attempt, exit
```

If `replay.tsv` does not exist or has only a header row, every rep
succeeded and no replay is needed.

::: {.callout-warning}

## Replay run

Canonical recovery command:

```bash
nextflow run main.nf -profile rockfish,replay \
    -resume \
    --output_dir ${OUTPUT} \
    --replay ${OUTPUT}/replay.tsv
```

The `replay` profile is defined at `nextflow.config:189–192`; it adds no
resource overrides today and exists so operators have a single canonical
spelling.

What the replay run does:

- **Filters fanout** to the 6-tuple `(species, group, nqtl, effect, h2,
  rep)` slots listed in the manifest (`main.nf:462`).
- **Filters sibling reps out of the channel before fanout.** Sibling reps
  in the same parameter combination will **not** appear as `CACHED` in
  the log — they do not appear at all. The replay-of-replay invocation
  (running the same manifest a second time with no replay-side failure)
  is the cache-hit oracle: all tasks report `CACHED`.
- **Cleans the partial DB rows** for each replayed slot before rewriting
  them. `DB_CLEAN_REPLAY_SLOTS` deletes 12 files per slot:

  | Count | Path pattern |
  |---|---|
  | 1 | `traits/{trait_id}.parquet` |
  | 1 | `traits/causal_variants/{trait_id}_causal.parquet` |
  | 1 | `traits/causal_genotypes/{trait_id}_causal_geno.parquet` |
  | 1 | `traits/phenotypes/{trait_id}_phenotype.parquet` |
  | 8 | `mappings/population={group}/mapping_id={mid}/{data,meta}.parquet` for each of the 4 `mode × pca` combos |

  Sibling reps, other parameter combinations, `marker_set_metadata/`,
  and `markers/` are not touched.

- **Runs the replayed slot with a fresh seed.** Causal-variant selection
  in `bin/create_causal_vars.py` and `bin/Create_Causal_QTLs.R` is
  intentionally non-deterministic (`np.random.default_rng()` is unseeded;
  the R script has no `set.seed()` call). The replayed rep is therefore
  drawn from a different realization than the failed attempt.


## Manage `replay.tsv` between runs

After a successful replay, the input `replay.tsv` is **not** archived,
emptied, or moved. The file persists on disk exactly as the first run
wrote it. Before any subsequent `-resume` invocation on the same
`output_dir`, archive or delete it:

```bash
mv ${OUTPUT}/replay.tsv ${OUTPUT}/replay.tsv.applied.$(date +%F)
```

Re-running with the same stale manifest is harmful: `DB_CLEAN_REPLAY_SLOTS`
re-deletes the files the previous replay just rewrote, and Nextflow's
work-dir cache then serves the downstream producers as `CACHED` — leaving
the DB with missing files. Treat `replay.tsv` as a one-shot input.

### Replay only within the scratch retention window

The replay relies on Nextflow's `-resume` cache hitting the work
directories of the first run's successful tasks. If the work dir has
been evicted from scratch, `-resume` cannot serve the prep-phase tasks
from cache; the prep phase re-runs, the sibling reps within the
replayed slot's parameter combination re-run under fresh seeds (because
causal-variant selection is non-deterministic), and the DB ends up with
cross-rep RNG inconsistency within that parameter combination.

If the work dir is gone, **do not** use `--replay`. Delete
`${OUTPUT}/db` and rerun the full pipeline.

### Statistical-validity caveat for replayed reps

Reps recovered via replay are conditioned on "did not trigger the failure
mode that dropped them." If the failure mode correlates with a
biologically-meaningful property of the simulation — for example, a low
MAF causing low Vp that pushes a rep into the
`GCTA_MAKE_GRM` upscaling failure path — replayed reps are not
exchangeable with first-run reps for the same parameter combination.

Before running `--replay`, inspect `replay.tsv` and confirm the failures
are not concentrated within a specific parameter combination of the
replication regime. If they are, the failure mode is correlated with
that combination's biology and replaying will bias its per-rep
statistics. See the same callout in
[Database Structure](database-structure.qmd) for the per-table
implication.

## See also

- [Database Structure](database-structure.qmd) — schema-level implication
  for per-rep aggregations under `--replay`.
- [Development Guide](development.qmd#testing-failure-handling-locally) —
  step-by-step recipe for exercising the failure → manifest → replay
  loop on `-profile test,docker`.
- `nemascan-sims-nf-notes/Development/error-handling/` — full design
  history, reviews (DS / NF engineer), and Step 15 V1–V8 validation
  evidence.
